channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree on ceasefire framework in US-led talks
item.description: The US announced an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire framework, but Hezbollah's absence leaves questions over enforcement.

item.title: US House votes to end Trump’s Iran war: Does it matter?
item.description: Four Republicans join Democrats in rare rebuke of president. But Congress still far from able to stop further attacks.

item.title: Did Germany lose its UNSC seat because of support for Israel?
item.description: Germany lost its Security Council bid to Austria and Portugal, which secured the two available seats.

item.title: Nepali Mount Everest guide found alive after being missing for six days
item.description: Dawa Sherpa was last seen around May 29 descending the mountain, but while he did not reach base camp, his client did.

item.title: Who is Khalilur Rahman, Bangladesh FM who beat Cyprus to UNGA presidency?
item.description: Rahman, a career diplomat who held several portfolios at the global body, beats Cyprus’s Ambassador Andreas Kakouris.

item.title: Video: US House of Representatives votes to block further war on Iran
item.description: Republican-led US House of Representatives passed a resolution to halt any further push of the war on Iran.

item.title: World Cup 2026: What are the key dates from the opening match to the final?
item.description: From must-watch games to the grand finale, here's a list of all the important dates on the 2026 World Cup schedule.

item.title: China bans four New Zealand lawmakers over visit to Taiwan
item.description: China says the MPs' visit sends the 'wrong signals' to Taiwan's ruling party.

item.title: Brunson scores 30 as Knicks upset Spurs in NBA Finals opener
item.description: Led by Jalen Brunson, the New York Knicks steal a road win against the San Antonio Spurs in game one of the NBA Finals.

item.title: Sabalenka ‘mentally off track’ as French Open exit made her want to ‘quit’
item.description: World number one Aryna Sabalenka let a two-set lead slip in a French Open quarterfinal defeat by Diana Shnaider.

item.title: How does football influence who we are?
item.description: How does football influence who we are?

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to conditional ceasefire
item.description: Deal is made through US-brokered talks in Washington, DC, that did not include Hezbollah.

item.title: Indonesia arrests officials in crackdown on corruption
item.description: Indonesia’s deputy minister for immigration affairs has been arrested after about 10 hours of questioning.

item.title: Iran war day 97: Tehran says no progress in talks; Israel attacks Lebanon
item.description: Israel carries out drone attacks on Lebanon after agreeing to renew ceasefire as Tehran defends attacks on Gulf nations.

item.title: What the US-Israel war on Iran will not change in the Middle East
item.description: The war may redraw alliances and shift balances of power, but geography, Palestine and political identity will endure.

item.title: Gaza is being offered coercion, not reconstruction
item.description: Mladenov’s plan turns rebuilding from a humanitarian obligation into a weapon of political control.

item.title: What’s behind the White House’s ‘Alien.gov’ website?
item.description: What’s behind the White House’s ‘Alien.gov’ website?

item.title: What are the Ebola vaccines under research, and when will they be ready?
item.description: At least three vaccines are being funded, research being fast-tracked as the outbreak spreads faster than the response.

item.title: How India’s CBSE exam scandal set off student outrage against PM Modi
item.description: India's largest school board has been mired in controversies, sparking demands that Modi's education minister resign.

item.title: World Cup 2026: FIFA bans fans from bringing reusable bottles to stadiums
item.description: Football's governing body makes a last-minute U-turn on its stadium code of conduct.

item.title: Germany admits failure to secure UNSC seat likely due to support for Israel
item.description: For the first time, Germany has failed to win a non-permanent seat on the UN Security Council.

item.title: Israeli strikes on residential buildings kill nine in Gaza City
item.description: Israeli strikes on residential buildings have killed nine Palestinians, setting homes ablaze and leaving destruction.

item.title: Indonesia’s rupiah falls to record low against US dollar
item.description: Currency plunge comes as energy shock from the Iran war casts a cloud over Southeast Asian economies.

item.title: UK far-right accused of exploiting killing of Henry Nowak
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer accused right-wing figures of exploiting the killing of Henry Nowak for political gain.

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX eyes $1.77tn valuation ahead of blockbuster IPO
item.description: Texas-based rocket company announces plans to sell shares at $135 apiece, aiming to raise approximately $75bn.
